name: Ph.D. in Computer Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: As a research doctorate the coherence rests on a research progression - graduate seminar (ECE 791), pre-doctoral research (ECE 792B), independent study I/II (ECE 725/726), and dissertation - alongside advanced electives (ECE 605, 657, 681, 690, 692) that sensibly span computer-engineering breadth (architecture, embedded, devices, networks). But only 11 courses are referenced and requirementGroups 0, so the qualifying-exam, breadth-requirement, and dissertation-credit structure that defines a Ph.D. is not visible on the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the doctoral milestone structure (qualifying exam, breadth coursework, dissertation proposal and defense) so the page reads as a research program rather than a short course list.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0, which is expected for a Ph.D. where students take advanced electives without formal chains, and missingCourseCount is only 1 - but that missing code, ECE 790A (doctoral dissertation and research), is the central research vehicle and its absence from the catalog is a meaningful dangling reference rather than a stray undergraduate prerequisite.
dimensions.suggestion: Restore/define ECE 790A in the catalog so the dissertation-research course the program depends on is not a dangling reference.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or doctoral competencies are stated in the data, which is a real gap for a Ph.D. where outcomes should describe independent research, publication, and dissertation-defense capabilities.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish doctoral learning outcomes framed as research competencies (original research, scholarly communication, teaching/mentoring) rather than course topics.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null and creditReconcileDelta is uncomputable (requirementGroups 0), so the post-master's coursework-versus-dissertation credit split (typically dissertation-dominated, e.g., ECE 792B/790A) is not shown.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credit requirement and the coursework-vs-dissertation-research distribution for the doctorate.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page is thin for a doctoral program: only 10 sampled courses, several of which are research placeholders (ECE 725/726 independent study, ECE 791 seminar, ECE 792B research), with no visible dissertation, qualifying-exam, or advising structure and no plan of study.
dimensions.suggestion: Expand the page with the doctoral timeline, expected research areas/faculty, and milestones instead of a small elective sample.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The sampled electives are reasonable but sparse and somewhat dated in emphasis - semiconductor devices (ECE 657), computer architecture (ECE 690), embedded systems (ECE 692), network functions (ECE 681), discrete-event systems (ECE 605), photovoltaics (ECE 618) - and do not foreground the AI-hardware, security, or quantum research frontiers a competitive CompE doctorate should signal.
dimensions.suggestion: Signal current research thrusts (AI accelerators, hardware security, quantum) via named advanced seminars so prospective doctoral students see the frontier the program works on.

descriptionQuality.justification: The sampled course descriptions that exist are substantive (e.g., ECE 690 on parallel architectures, ECE 692 on the embedded design process), but many entries are research placeholders (ECE 725/726/791/792B) whose descriptions are procedural rather than content-bearing.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Consistent with a Ph.D., most courses carry null prerequisites (deepestPrereqChain 0), which is appropriate; the one clarity issue is that ECE 790A, the dissertation-research course, is referenced but missing from the catalog.

missingTopics.rationale: The elective sample (ECE 690/692/657) covers classical architecture and devices but omits the ML-accelerator and AI-systems research now central to computer-engineering doctorates.
With architecture (ECE 690) and device (ECE 657) courses but no hardware-security seminar, the program does not surface a major CompE research area.
A doctoral program building on semiconductor devices (ECE 657) should expose students to quantum-device and post-Moore computing research.
There is no verification/EDA research course despite VLSI/architecture strength elsewhere in the department, a gap for design-automation doctoral work.

missingTopics.topic: Research methods and responsible conduct of research
missingTopics.rationale: Beyond the seminar (ECE 791), no course addresses research methodology, scholarly writing, or RCR/ethics expected in doctoral training.

emergingTrends.trend: AI accelerators, neuromorphic, and in-memory computing
emergingTrends.rationale: Purpose-built AI hardware is the leading edge of computer-engineering research; advanced seminars should build on ECE 690 toward accelerator architecture.
Quantum information is a fast-growing doctoral frontier that ECE 657's device foundation could extend into.
Trusted hardware and side-channel research are expanding; a seminar extending ECE 681/690 would position doctoral students at this frontier.
Post-Moore scaling favors domain-specific and edge accelerators; ECE 692 embedded research could evolve toward on-device AI architectures.
Doctoral training increasingly emphasizes ethics, reproducibility, and trustworthiness; the seminar (ECE 791) could formalize an RCR component.

topSuggestions: Publish the doctoral milestone structure (qualifying exam, breadth requirements, dissertation proposal/defense) and program learning outcomes framed as research competencies.
State the total credit requirement and the coursework-vs-dissertation-research split now hidden by null statedTotalCredits.
Restore/define the missing ECE 790A dissertation-research course so the central research vehicle is not a dangling reference.
Expand the thin catalog page with research thrusts and faculty areas signaling AI-hardware, security, and quantum frontiers.
Add advanced research seminars in AI accelerators, hardware security, and formal methods to modernize the elective set beyond ECE 657/690/692.
